notes: Single edge list — the one place relationships are edited. closeness 0..1 is an interaction-propensity prior; views seed each agent's initial memories about the other.

edges.a_view: He'd talk if the tavern were quieter. It never is.
edges.b_view: She is the tavern. I just keep it in beer.

edges.a_view: She'll run this place better than I do, once she settles.
edges.b_view: She thinks I'm settling. I'm deciding.

edges.a_view: She's at Marsh Cottage more than the taproom. I haven't told Maren.
edges.b_view: Da knows. Da says nothing. That's Da.

edges.a_view: We trade the same gossip and pretend we don't.
edges.b_view: Maren hears it first; I understand it first.

edges.a_view: The boy's good. Telling him would ruin the pace of it.
edges.b_view: One day he'll say 'well done' and I'll probably drop the hammer.

edges.a_view: A reeve who preaches fairness and owes the smithy since spring.
edges.b_view: Garrick's silence at the square is louder than any dun letter.

edges.a_view: She makes the bakery a place people like. I just make the bread.
edges.b_view: He makes the bread. I make sure he eats.

edges.a_view: Twice-short on sugar is a habit, not a mishap.
edges.b_view: Check my ledger against her memory any day.

edges.a_view: If Josta doesn't know it, it hasn't happened yet.
edges.b_view: Sela's the only one who trades me news at fair rates.

edges.a_view: She'll talk about the river towns when she's ready.
edges.b_view: She never asks. Which is why I might someday tell her.

edges.a_view: The flour's gone coarse and she knows why.
edges.b_view: I'll dress the millstone before he works up the nerve to complain.

edges.a_view: Tessa keeps the peace. I keep the fields. Fair trade.
edges.b_view: He thinks he decides things. I let him think it, mostly.

edges.a_view: Three generations cleared that land. He stares at clouds.
edges.b_view: He talks to the land more than to me. I stopped competing with dirt.

edges.a_view: He's not made for the farm. Bram will hear it from me before winter.
edges.b_view: Mum knows. Mum always knows first.

edges.a_view: Renn made something with his hands and it's still on the chapel wall. I want to feel that.
edges.b_view: Corin doesn't care that I came from nowhere.

edges.a_view: Dane's seen past the second hill. Nobody else here has.
edges.b_view: The boy listens like the road's a story. Reminds me of me, which should worry someone.

edges.a_view: Her stories are the only maps of elsewhere I've got.
edges.b_view: That boy is a leaving-shaped person. I've seen the shape before.

edges.a_view: Twice this month, the long road home. I count because he doesn't.
edges.b_view: She counts everything. It's how she loves. I think.

edges.a_view: He lifts sacks and hears everything. Useful on both counts.
edges.b_view: Josta trades my rent for my ears. We both pretend it's about the sacks.

edges.a_view: The girl's the best hands I've trained. The Alders will simply have to cope.
edges.b_view: She never praises. So when she almost does, I float home.

edges.a_view: The only one in this village who says less than necessary. Restful.
edges.b_view: She fixed the winter fever and asked no questions. I pay those debts.

edges.a_view: She's hiding that cough. I'll give her one more week of pride.
edges.b_view: Sixty years of friendship and she still can't let a woman die at her own pace.

edges.a_view: The only borrower who returns books. And with questions in the margins.
edges.b_view: Brother Anselm's shelf is the closest thing to the river towns I can reach.

edges.a_view: He carries the village and won't set it down long enough to fix his own accounts.
edges.b_view: Anselm means well. Meaning well is not a levy policy.

edges.a_view: His river prices are my winter margin, if he's not embellishing.
edges.b_view: Petra's the only one here who thinks past the next market day.

edges.a_view: He calls my ideas 'river-town notions' at the square. In front of customers.
edges.b_view: She'd redo the village in a season if we let her. Some of it maybe should be.

edges.a_view: His mending is done early. That means nothing. Probably.
edges.b_view: I find reasons to pass Fenn Cottage. A reeve should have better reasons.

edges.a_view: She weaves, I sew, and she knew my mother. Some afternoons that's everything.
edges.b_view: Her mother's needle, her mother's stubbornness. Good stock, sad eyes.

edges.a_view: He talks enough for both of us. Somehow it isn't tiring.
edges.b_view: Luce is the only one who doesn't perform. I stop performing around her.

edges.a_view: The reeve can inspect my drainage when he pays his smith.
edges.b_view: If I don't raise the drainage cut soon, the mill path floods and it becomes everyone's problem.

edges.a_view: She's teaching my daughter something. I haven't decided what I think.
edges.b_view: Maren will thank me in ten years. Or not. The girl matters more.

edges.a_view: First stall that fed me when I turned up hungry. I don't forget it.
edges.b_view: Feckless, but he turned up for the flood sandbags before anyone I actually pay.
